name: Oral Communication
description: GE Area A1: Oral Communication. Choose from: COMM 110, etc.

name: Written Communication
description: GE Area A2: Written Communication. Choose from: ENGL 100, etc.

name: Critical Thinking
description: GE Area A3: Critical Thinking. Choose from: PHIL 270, etc.

name: Physical Science
description: GE Area B1: Physical Science. For CS majors, typically PHYS 151.

name: Life Science
description: GE Area B2: Life Science. Choose from: BIOL 200, etc.

name: Lab Activity
description: GE Area B3: Laboratory Activity. Typically paired with B1 or B2.
notes: Lab paired with B1 or B2

name: Math/Quantitative Reasoning
description: GE Area B4: Satisfied by MATH 122 (Calculus I) for CS majors.
notes: Satisfied by MATH 122 for CS majors

name: Science Elective
description: GE Area B5: Additional science or math course beyond B1-B4.
notes: Upper division science for some students

name: Arts
description: GE Area C1: Arts. Choose from: ART 100, MUSC 100, THEA 113, etc.

name: Humanities
description: GE Area C2: Humanities. Choose from: ENGL 180, PHIL 100, etc.

name: Additional C (Arts/Humanities)
description: GE Area C3: Additional Arts or Humanities course.
notes: Any approved C1 or C2 course

name: Social Sciences
description: GE Area D1: Social Sciences. Choose from: PSY 100, SOC 100, POSC 100, etc.

name: Additional Social Science
description: GE Area D2: Second social science course from a different department.

name: US History & Government
description: GE Area D3: American History, Constitution, and Government.

name: US Government/State & Local
description: GE Area D4: American Institutions — US & California Government.

name: Lifelong Understanding
description: GE Area E: Lifelong Understanding & Self-Development.

name: Ethnic Studies
description: GE Area F: Ethnic Studies. Required per CA AB 1460.

name: Upper Division GE: Science
description: Upper Division GE in Area B. Must be taken after 60 units.

name: Upper Division GE: Arts/Humanities
description: Upper Division GE in Area C. Must be taken after 60 units.

name: Upper Division GE: Social Science
description: Upper Division GE in Area D. Must be taken after 60 units.

name: Mechanics and Heat
description: First course in introductory calculus-based physics. Kinematics, forces and Newton's laws, conservation of energy and momentum, collisions, angular momentum, engines, entropy.
notes: Prerequisite/Corequisite: MATH 122. Science sequence option — also satisfies GE B1

name: Electricity and Magnetism
description: Calculus-based physics: electricity, magnetism, optics, waves.
notes: Science sequence option — completes PHYS 151+152 sequence

name: General Chemistry
description: First semester of a two-semester sequence. Introduction to principles of chemistry including chemical reactions, stoichiometry, thermochemistry, electronic structure, bonding, and properties of solids, liquids, gases, and solutions.
notes: Science sequence option. Prereq/Coreq: MATH 112B or higher

name: General Biology
description: Brief survey of major areas of biology including cell biology, genetics, evolution, phylogeny, plant and animal anatomy and physiology, ecology, and behavior. Designed for non-science majors.
notes: Science elective option. Prereqs: Completion of GE Area 2 and at least one GE Subject Area 1 course

name: Human Biology
description: Brief survey of human biology focusing on anatomy, physiology, and development of cells, tissues, organs, and organ systems; including molecular biology, genetics, ecology, evolution, and diversity.
notes: Science elective option. Prereqs: Completion of GE Area 2 and at least one GE Subject Area 1 course

name: Human Physiology
description: General introduction to the functional integration of human body systems. Designed for majors in biomedical engineering, physical education, and the allied health fields.
notes: Science elective option. Prereqs: Completion of GE Area 2 and at least one GE Subject Area 1 course

name: Introduction to the Engineering Profession
description: Freshman orientation seminar on careers in engineering. Speakers from various fields illustrate opportunities and challenges in the engineering profession.
notes: Prereq/Coreq: MATH 111 or MATH 113 or MATH 122

name: Academic Success Skills
description: Development of skills and identification of strengths and weaknesses for success in a COE major.

name: Computers, Ethics, and Society
description: Examination of the social impact of information technologies. Survey of technology, ethical obligations of specialists, computer in the workplace, shifts of power, privacy, and legal issues related to computing.
notes: Requires completion of at least 60 units

name: Scientific Research Communication
description: Introduction to technical writing for students pursuing research careers. Accessing and using research literature. Writing technical and research reports. Oral presentation of research and scientific information.
notes: ENGR Communication choice — ENGR 361 or ENGR 390. Prereq: GE foundation courses

name: Information and Communication Technology for Sustainability
description: Information and communication technology and Sustainability. Concepts for understanding and analyzing sustainability of engineering contexts. Climate change, smart systems, gamification. Systems thinking.
notes: ENGR Communication choice — ENGR 361 or ENGR 390. Prereq: upper-division standing, GE Foundation. Coreq: ENGR 350

name: Probability and Statistics with Applications to Computing
description: Applications of probability and statistics to computing. Random variables, probability distributions, Markov chains, Bayesian networks, sampling techniques, hypothesis testing, regression and analytic methods.
notes: Probability & Statistics choice — CECS 381 or E E 381

name: Cybersecurity in Business
description: Foundation and business applications of cybersecurity. Principles and methodologies of risk assessment and management. Assurance and internal control. Security operations and administration.
